text: When the primary amine-thiourea organocatalysis concept was introduced in 2006, a growing body of literature has demonstrated that bifunctional catalysts based on this theme are highly efficient for numerous asymmetric transformations leading to complex chiral organic molecules. Today, the use of primary amine-based thioureas as organocatalysts in asymmetric catalysis has demonstrated their versatile application in achieving a plethora of highly enantioselective reactions. This article aims to give an account of the tremendous progresses that have been achieved in this field and to highlight the increasing importance of primary amine-based thioureas as organocatalysts in synthetic chemistry.
explanation: The text is very generic and contains broad statements without specific data or citations, a pattern often seen in AI-generated abstracts, though it could also be human-written.
